heading: Capital requirements for certain acquisition, development, or construction loans
label: 1831bb Capital requirements for certain acquisition, development, or construction loans

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The appropriate Federal banking agencies may only require a depository institution to assign a heightened risk weight to a high volatility commercial real estate (HVCRE) exposure (as such term is defined under section 324.2 of title 12, Code of Federal Regulations, as of , or if a successor regulation is in effect as of , such term or any successor term contained in such successor regulation) under any risk-based capital requirement if such exposure is an HVCRE ADC loan.October 11, 2017May 24, 2018

children.label: A
children.text: primarily finances, has financed, or refinances the acquisition, development, or construction of real property;

children.label: B
children.text: has the purpose of providing financing to acquire, develop, or improve such real property into income-producing real property; and

children.label: C
children.text: is dependent upon future income or sales proceeds from, or refinancing of, such real property for the repayment of such credit facility;

children.label: i
children.text: one- to four-family residential properties;

children.label: ii
children.text: real property that would qualify as an investment in community development; or

children.label: iii
children.text: agricultural land;

children.label: B
children.text: the acquisition or refinance of existing income-producing real property secured by a mortgage on such property, if the cash flow being generated by the real property is sufficient to support the debt service and expenses of the real property, in accordance with the institution’s applicable loan underwriting criteria for permanent financings;

children.label: C
children.text: improvements to existing income-producing improved real property secured by a mortgage on such property, if the cash flow being generated by the real property is sufficient to support the debt service and expenses of the real property, in accordance with the institution’s applicable loan underwriting criteria for permanent financings; or

children.label: i
children.text: the loan-to-value ratio is less than or equal to the applicable maximum supervisory loan-to-value ratio as determined by the appropriate Federal banking agency;

children.label: I
children.text: cash;

children.label: II
children.text: unencumbered readily marketable assets;

children.label: III
children.text: paid development expenses out-of-pocket; or

children.label: IV
children.text: contributed real property or improvements; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the borrower contributed the minimum amount of capital described under clause (ii) before the depository institution advances funds (other than the advance of a nominal sum made in order to secure the depository institution’s lien against the real property) under the credit facility, and such minimum amount of capital contributed by the borrower is contractually required to remain in the project until the credit facility has been reclassified by the depository institution as a non-HVCRE ADC loan under subsection (d);
does not include any loan made prior to ; andJanuary 1, 2015
does not include a credit facility reclassified as a non-HVCRE ADC loan under subsection (d).

children.heading: Value of contributed real property
children.label: c Value of contributed real property
children.text: For purposes of this section, the value of any real property contributed by a borrower as a capital contribution shall be the appraised value of the property as determined under standards prescribed pursuant to , in connection with the extension of the credit facility or loan to such borrower.section 3339 of this title

children.heading: Reclassification as a Non-HVRCE ADC loan
children.label: d Reclassification as a Non-HVRCE ADC loan

children.text: the substantial completion of the development or construction of the real property being financed by the credit facility; and
cash flow being generated by the real property being sufficient to support the debt service and expenses of the real property,

children.heading: Existing authorities
children.label: e Existing authorities
children.text: Nothing in this section shall limit the supervisory, regulatory, or enforcement authority of an appropriate Federal banking agency to further the safe and sound operation of an institution under the supervision of the appropriate Federal banking agency.
